name: San Salvador Way
name_original: Parque Márgenes del río Bernesga y Plaza de San Marcos – circular por León
description: Hike an easy 3.1-mile (4.9 km) circular route along the Bernesga River in León, passing the historic San Marcos bridge and Parador.
region_intro: Hiking around León offers access to diverse and dramatic natural landscapes, from the high-mountain scenery of the Picos de Europa National Park to the unique ancient Roman gold mining landscape of Las Médulas. The province is characterized by the rugged Leonese Cantabrian Mountains, which include several Biosphere Reserves featuring deep gorges, ancient forests, and picturesque valleys. Hikers can explore a varied terrain of limestone peaks, river valleys, and expansive natural parks. Best hiking trails around León The most popular hiking route is French Way: León City Loop, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete. This easy route offers an urban exploration of the historic city, forming part of the renowned Camino de Santiago. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parque de la Candamia y Las Lomas – circular by León, an easy 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This route winds through parkland and gentle hills, providing accessible natural scenery close to the city. Local hikers also love the San Salvador Way, a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail leading through urban and suburban areas, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Hiking around León is defined by dramatic mountain ranges like the Picos de Europa, unique geological formations such as Las Médulas, and the extensive network of trails within the Cantabrian Mountains. The region offers routes suitable for various ability levels, from easy city loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in León are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 160 reviews. Nearly 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore León's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in León?
A: León offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 120 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy city loops to more challenging mountain ascents, catering to all ability levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for families in León?
A: Yes, León has numerous easy trails perfect for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047218108/parque-de-la-candamia-y-las-lomas-circular-by-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de la Candamia y Las Lomas – circular by León&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4-mile path through parkland and gentle hills close to the city. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051977722/french-way-leon-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way: León City Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another accessible option, offering an urban exploration of the historic city.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain hikes in León?
A: Absolutely. León is home to dramatic mountain ranges like the Picos de Europa, offering challenging alpine routes. Areas like the Ubiña massif in Babia, Pico Gilbo, and Pico Yordas in the Riaño and Mampodre Mountain Regional Park provide strenuous climbs and breathtaking views for experienced hikers.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to explore while hiking in León?
A: León boasts spectacular natural features. The &lt;strong&gt;Picos de Europa National Park&lt;/strong&gt; offers the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Liordes Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; with stunning vistas. The ancient Roman gold mining landscape of &lt;strong&gt;Las Médulas&lt;/strong&gt; is a UNESCO World Heritage site with unique reddish hills. You can also explore the geological formations of the &lt;strong&gt;Vegacervera Gorges&lt;/strong&gt; and the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Valporquero Cave&lt;/strong&gt; in Los Argüellos.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in León?
A: Yes, León is home to several beautiful waterfalls. The famous &lt;strong&gt;Nocedo Waterfall&lt;/strong&gt; (also known as Cola de Caballo de Nocedo) in Valdepiélago and the &lt;strong&gt;La Gualta Waterfall and Medicinal Springs&lt;/strong&gt; in Noceda del Bierzo are popular spots for hikers seeking scenic cascades.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in León?
A: Many trails in León are dog-friendly, especially in natural parks and rural areas. However, it's always advisable to check specific park regulations, particularly in protected areas like the Picos de Europa National Park, as rules can vary regarding leash requirements or restricted zones for wildlife protection.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in León?
A: Yes, many popular routes in León are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047218108/parque-de-la-candamia-y-las-lomas-circular-by-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de la Candamia y Las Lomas – circular by León&lt;/a&gt; is a well-liked option. The &lt;strong&gt;Faedo de Ciñera&lt;/strong&gt; also offers a beautiful circular route through an ancient beech forest, and the &lt;strong&gt;Pico Gilbo Loop Trail&lt;/strong&gt; provides stunning views of the Riaño reservoir.

Q: What are the public transport options for reaching hiking areas in León?
A: Public transport to some major hiking areas, especially within the Picos de Europa or more remote Biosphere Reserves, can be limited. While some towns are accessible by bus, reaching specific trailheads often requires a car. For areas closer to León city, local buses might serve some starting points. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for your specific destination.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in León?
A: Parking availability varies by location. Popular sites like Las Médulas or the Faedo de Ciñera typically have designated parking areas, though they can fill up during peak season. For more remote mountain trailheads, parking might be less formal or limited. Always arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in León?
A: The best time for hiking in León generally depends on the type of trail. Spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for most routes. Summer (July-August) is excellent for high-altitude mountain trails, but lower elevation routes can be hot. Winter (November-March) is suitable for lower-level walks, but higher elevations may require snowshoes or specialized gear due to snow and ice.

Q: Are there any unique historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails in León?
A: León's rich history is intertwined with its landscapes. The &lt;strong&gt;Las Médulas&lt;/strong&gt; site, an ancient Roman gold mine, is a prime example. Within León city, you can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5658871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman City Walls&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4590991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum&lt;/a&gt;, many of which can be incorporated into urban walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24636079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum – Plaza del Grano loop from León&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in León?
A: The hiking routes in León are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse and dramatic natural landscapes, from the high-mountain scenery to unique geological formations, and the extensive network of trails suitable for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in certain areas of León?
A: Generally, permits are not required for most hiking trails in León. However, for specific activities or access to certain protected areas, especially within National Parks like Picos de Europa, it's always wise to check with the park authorities for any specific regulations or permits that might be needed, particularly for overnight stays in refuges or access to sensitive ecological zones.
tips: Excellent area on Paseo Salamanca, with a bike lane and a walk.
Pedestrian walkway of 80 meters. of light on the river Bernesga in León.
In the square is the Convent of San Marcos, home to the Museum of León, with remains and pieces of great historical-artistic value.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows the banks of the Bernesga River, offering a relatively flat and urban-friendly terrain. You'll find paths suitable for walking, jogging, and cycling, making it an accessible experience.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. As it's an urban park setting, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash and that you clean up after them to maintain a pleasant environment for all users.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy views of the Bernesga River and pass by several notable features. Highlights include the Bernesga Riverbank Promenade, the Pedestrian Bridge over the Bernesga River, and the historic Parador de San Marcos. The San Marcos bridge is particularly significant as a historical passage for pilgrims.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: This urban trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for walking, while summer evenings can be lovely. Even in winter, the flat, paved paths make it accessible, though you might want to dress warmly.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee to access the park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Parque Márgenes del río Bernesga or the Plaza de San Marcos. It's a public urban park and promenade, freely accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As this route is integrated into the city of León, you can typically find street parking in the surrounding urban areas near the Bernesga River or Plaza de San Marcos. There are also public parking facilities within walking distance of the park.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The route's easy difficulty, flat terrain, and urban setting make it very family-friendly. The park also includes children's play areas, adding to its appeal for families.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other significant paths. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the Camino del Salvador, the Camino Francés - 06 León a Cacabelos, Puente de San Marcos, Calle Ausente, and Calle Primero de Mayo.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: Given its location within the city of León, there are numerous cafes, restaurants, and other amenities available in the vicinity of the park and Plaza de San Marcos. You'll find plenty of options for refreshments before or after your walk.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect?
faqs.answer: You can expect a pleasant blend of urban green spaces and river views. The route offers scenic perspectives of the Bernesga River, which is a central feature, along with recreational areas and historical bridges, all within the city environment.

faqs.question: Is the route accessible for strollers or wheelchairs?
faqs.answer: Yes, due to its primarily flat and paved nature along the riverbanks, this route is generally accessible for strollers and wheelchairs, making it a good option for a wider range of visitors.

waypoints.name: Pedestrian Bridge over the Bernesga River
